title: RapidMeta Renal | Iptacopan (Factor B Inhibitor) in IgA Nephropathy v1.0

pico.pop: Adults with biopsy-confirmed primary IgA nephropathy with persistent proteinuria (UPCR >=1.0 g/g) on stable maximally-tolerated RAAS blockade
pico.int: Iptacopan 200 mg orally twice daily (factor B inhibitor of the alternative complement pathway)
pico.comp: Placebo on top of supportive care (RAAS blockade)
pico.out: Percent reduction in 24-h UPCR at month 9 (interim primary); time-weighted average eGFR slope at month 24 (final primary)
pico.subgroup: Baseline UPCR (>=1.5 vs <1.5 g/g), prior immunosuppression, race

real_data_entries.name: APPLAUSE-IgAN
real_data_entries.group: Adults with primary IgAN on RAS blockade, iptacopan 200 mg BID vs placebo (UPCR ratio 9-mo interim primary; eGFR slope 24-mo final primary)

allOutcomes.title: UPCR ratio at month 9 (iptacopan / placebo, geometric mean ratio; interim primary)
Annualised eGFR slope at month 24 (iptacopan minus placebo; mL/min/1.73m^2/year, MD; final primary)

real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Rizk DV et al. N Engl J Med 2024 (APPLAUSE-IgAN, 9-mo interim UPCR primary).
